description: A lightweight introductory course for developers and operations engineers encountering DevOps for the first time. Covers core concepts and culture, the workflow from code to production (the CI/CD lifecycle), and a tour of the leading open-source tools used at each stage. The focus is on building a clear big picture quickly rather than deep hands-on practice.
summary: Three concise lectures covering what DevOps is and why it matters (concepts and culture), how code flows from a developer's machine to users (the CI/CD lifecycle), and the key open-source tools at each stage of that workflow.
target_audience: Beginners coming to DevOps for the first time — developers or operations engineers who have heard terms like containers and CI/CD but want to understand the full picture.

lectures.title: What Is DevOps? Concepts and Culture (CALMS)
objectives: Understand the problems caused by separating development (Dev) and operations (Ops), and the core values DevOps pursues: fast delivery, stability, and collaboration.
Recognize that DevOps is not a tool but a culture and a way of working.
Explain DevOps principles using the CALMS framework (Culture, Automation, Lean, Measurement, Sharing).

lectures.title: The DevOps Workflow — From Code to Production (the CI/CD Lifecycle)
objectives: Trace the full DevOps lifecycle — Plan → Code → Build → Test → Release → Deploy → Operate → Monitor — and explain how it loops continuously.
Understand the role of CI (Continuous Integration) and CD (Continuous Delivery/Deployment) within the workflow and the difference between them.
Explain how version control, automated pipelines, and feedback loops connect into a single end-to-end flow.

lectures.title: DevOps Open-Source Tools — A Tour of the Ecosystem by Stage
objectives: Identify the leading open-source tools that correspond to each stage of the DevOps workflow: version control, CI/CD, containers, orchestration, IaC, and monitoring.
Describe in one sentence what problem each of Git, Jenkins/GitLab CI, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform/Ansible, and Prometheus/Grafana solves.
Identify which tools and topics to study next after completing this introduction.
